title: 404 error when accessing magic link
symptom: When trying to access the magic link, the user receives a 404 error indicating the page does not exist.
applies_when: Using a magic link with a token
not_this_if: The correct page route is used
The token is valid and the page exists
root_cause: The 404 error occurs because the URL is pointing to a non-existent page ('magic-link-auth') instead of the intended page ('update_password'). This mismatch leads to the server not finding the requested resource.
fix_steps: Change the URL to point to the correct page route, which is 'update_password'.
Ensure that the query parameter 'token' is included in the URL.
Create a query parameter variable called 'token' on the 'update_password' page to store the token value.
agent_instruction: Update the magic link URL to point to 'update_password' instead of 'magic-link-auth'.
